summary: Scorching Scarlet>Fire>Playing>DARK STAR chain, lush Space jam
blurb: Second set erupts with a seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire → Playing In The Band → Dark Star → Terrapin Station marathon and a soaring Space jam. Jerry’s lightning‑bolt guitar cuts through a pronounced kick drum while Vince delivers lush keyboards. Highlights include Hell In A Bucket, the dark star excursion and the fiery Terrapin.
review: On September 13, 1993 at The Spectrum the Dead delivered one of their most electrifying late‑career outings. The first set was solid, opening with a rollicking "Hell In A Bucket" that set an upbeat tone, but it was the second set that truly defined the night. A blistering segue chain—Scarlet Begonias into Fire On The Mountain, then into Playing In The Band, spiraling into Dark Star and finally bursting through Terrapin Station—had fans chanting “Lightning‑Bolt!” as Jerry’s guitar screamed with a liquid, ripping tone. Vince Welnick’s keyboards added a gorgeous, Tubular Bells‑style texture that wove through the jam, especially during the expansive Space section where Mickey and Bill’s drumming grabbed you by the throat. The rhythm section was anchored by a very pronounced kick drum that some found initially intrusive but ultimately gave the performance extra drive. While Easy Answers and Days Between fell flat for a few listeners, the overall energy stayed high through the encore, even if "I Fought The Law" never quite matched the set’s intensity. Multiple soundboard captures confirm the show’s fire‑filled vibe, with the Miller SBD (gd1993-09-13.sbd.miller.34030.flac16) delivering pristine clarity and capturing the Spectrum’s ambience, making it the definitive listening source.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are clearly positive, two are mixed but not negative; reviewers repeatedly praise the Scarlet>Fire>Playing>DARK STAR sequence, Jerry’s lightning‑bolt tone, and Vince’s lush keyboards while noting only minor criticisms of a few songs. The consistent high praise across multiple sources warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Pristine soundboard capture with clear, balanced mix and minimal dropouts; highlights the Spectrum ambience and showcases the band’s fire‑filled performance.

key_highlights: Seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire → Playing In The Band → Dark Star → Terrapin Station marathon
Lush Space jam with extended drum work from Mickey and Bill
Vince Welnick’s Tubular Bells‑style keyboard textures and solid accompaniment
Pronounced kick drum adds power; overall high‑energy second set

band_performance.Jerry: Lightning‑bolt guitar tone rippling through Scarlet, Fire and Dark Star; strong runs and an especially ripping solo on Terrapin Station, with a polarizing but interesting MIDI acoustic texture noted by some listeners.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation throughout, roaring low end on the encore and generally anchoring the jam-heavy second set.
band_performance.Bob: Prominent rhythm guitar that holds its own alongside Vince’s keys; vocal delivery strong, especially on Fire On The Mountain.
band_performance.Vince: Lush, pretty‑damn‑good keyboards; Tubular Bells‑style jam with Jerry adds chills, and a spacious texture fuels the Space improvisation.
band_performance.Mickey: Drumming shines during the space segment, delivering gripping, throat‑grabbing fills that elevate the jam.
band_performance.Billy: Very pronounced kick drum drives the band forward; overall solid drumming complements Mickey’s textures.
